title: Monash Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarship 2026 – Fully Funded Master’s and PhD Opportunity for International Students
summary: The Monash Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarship 2026 is one of the most competitive and prestigious fully funded graduate research scholarships available in Australia. Funded by the Australian Government and administered by Monash University, this scholarship supports outstanding domestic and international students pursuing Master s by Research or Doctoral (PhD) degrees. The RTP Scholarship is designed to attract high-performing students with strong academic backgrounds and significant research potential. Through this program, scholars gain the opportunity to conduct advanced research, collaborate with world-class academics, and contribute to groundbreaking discoveries in multiple fields. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); This scholarship not only provides comprehensive financial support but also enables scholars to focus entirely on their research without financial stress. By covering tuition costs and offering a generous living allowance, the program helps talented researchers from around the world build their academic and professional careers in Australia. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Overview of the Monash RTP Scholarship 2026 The Research Training Program (RTP) is a government-funded initiative in Australia that supports postgraduate research training at Australian universities. The scholarship is awarded to students enrolled in graduate research degrees, including PhD programs and research-based Master s degrees. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The program at Monash University aims to develop the next generation of global researchers by providing funding, mentorship, and access to state-of-the-art research facilities. The scholarship is highly competitive, with selections based on academic excellence, research achievements, and the applicant s potential to contribute to the global research community. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Financial Benefits of the Monash RTP Scholarship 2026 The Monash RTP Scholarship is a fully funded opportunity that provides significant financial support to recipients throughout their research studies. 1. Annual Living Allowance (Stipend) Successful applicants receive a living stipend of approximately AUD $37,145 per year (2026 rate) to cover living expenses while studying in Australia. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); 2. Full Tuition Fee Coverage The RTP includes a tuition fee offset, meaning the scholarship covers the cost of tuition for the entire duration of the research program. 3. Relocation Allowance Eligible international students may receive financial assistance to help cover relocation expenses when moving to Australia to begin their studies. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); 4. Additional Scholarship Packaging In some cases, the RTP stipend may be combined with other Monash-funded scholarships to enhance the financial support package for international students. 5. Access to Research Facilities Recipients gain access to world-class laboratories, academic mentors, and professional development opportunities within Monash University. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Duration of the Scholarship The duration of the Monash RTP Scholarship depends on the level of study: Doctoral (PhD) Programs: Up to 3.5 years Master s by Research Programs: Typically 2 years Students are expected to remain enrolled as full-time research candidates throughout the scholarship period. Eligible Fields of Study The Monash RTP Scholarship supports research across all academic disciplines offered by Monash University. Examples of research areas include: Engineering and Technology Medical and Health Sciences Science and Biotechnology Business and Economics Education Social Sciences Information Technology Environmental Science Law and Public Policy Because the scholarship covers all research fields, students from diverse academic backgrounds can apply. Who Can Apply? (Eligible Countries and Regions) One of the major advantages of this scholarship is its global accessibility. Eligible Nationalities Applicants can come from: All countries worldwide Domestic applicants in Australia International students from Africa, Asia, Europe, the Americas, and the Middle East ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Specifically, the scholarship is open to: Australian citizens New Zealand citizens Australian permanent residents Australian humanitarian visa holders International students from any country This makes the RTP Scholarship an excellent opportunity for students from developing countries seeking high-quality research training abroad. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the Monash RTP Scholarship 2026, applicants must meet several academic and institutional requirements. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); 1. Academic Excellence Applicants must demonstrate outstanding academic performance in their previous degrees. 2. Research Potential Candidates should have strong research capabilities, often demonstrated through: Research projects Publications Academic achievements Prior research experience 3. Admission to a Graduate Research Program Applicants must apply for admission into a Master s by Research or PhD program at Monash University. 4. English Language Proficiency International applicants must meet the English language requirements set by Monash University. 5. Competitive Selection Process Scholarships are awarded based on a competitive evaluation process considering: Academic performance Research output Research proposal quality Academic references Only candidates with exceptional research potential typically receive the award. Required Documents for Application Applicants must prepare and submit several documents as part of their scholarship application. Typical documents include: Academic curriculum vitae (CV) Official academic transcripts and certificates Proof of citizenship or residency English language proficiency test results Detailed research proposal Academic references Invitation to apply (if required by faculty) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); All documents must be submitted in English or accompanied by certified translations. Application Process for the Monash RTP Scholarship 2026 Applying for the RTP Scholarship at Monash University involves a structured application process. Step 1: Identify a Research Area Applicants should identify their preferred research area and explore available graduate research programs. Step 2: Contact a Potential Supervisor Many faculties recommend contacting a supervisor before submitting the application. Step 3: Apply for Graduate Research Admission Applicants must submit an application for admission to a research degree through the Monash online portal. Step 4: Automatic Scholarship Consideration Students who apply for graduate research admission are automatically considered for available graduate research scholarships, including the RTP. Step 5: Wait for Assessment Results Scholarship decisions are typically announced several weeks after the application deadline. Apply here Key Application Deadlines Monash offers four scholarship rounds each year. International Applicants Round 1 Deadline: 31st March 2026 Round 3 Deadline: 31st August 2026 Domestic Applicants Round 2 Deadline: 31st May 2026 Round 4 Deadline: 31st October 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit applications early because the scholarship is highly competitive. Selection Process The selection process for the RTP Scholarship is rigorous and merit-based. Evaluation typically considers: Academic results from previous degrees Research experience Publications and research output Quality of the research proposal Academic referee reports ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Because the scholarship is competitive, only candidates with strong academic profiles and significant research potential are selected. Why Study at Monash University? Monash University is one of Australia s leading research institutions and consistently ranks among the world s top universities. Key advantages include: Globally recognized research programs Advanced research infrastructure Strong international collaborations Opportunities to work with leading academics Access to global research networks Studying at Monash also provides students with exposure to a diverse academic environment and opportunities to participate in international research initiatives. Deadline International Applicants (Round 1): 31st March 2026 The Monash Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarship 2026represents an exceptional opportunity for students seeking fully funded postgraduate research training in Australia. By covering tuition fees, providing a generous stipend, and offering access to world-class research resources, the scholarship allows talented individuals from around the world to pursue advanced academic careers. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Monash Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarship 2026 – Fully Funded Master’s and PhD Opportunity for International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Norad Renewable Energy Grant 2026: Up to Millions in Funding for Private Companies Developing Renewable Energy Projects in Developing Countries
summary: The Norwegian Agency for Development Cooperation (Norad) has launched the Support for Enterprise Development in Renewable Energy in Developing Countries 2026 Stage 1 (Insights) call for proposals. This initiative is part of a broader effort by the Norwegian government to strengthen private sector participation in renewable energy development across emerging and developing economies. The program provides grant funding to commercial enterprises developing renewable energy projects that can increase energy access, stimulate economic growth, and support the global transition to sustainable energy systems. The funding focuses on reducing financial risks during the early stages of project development and preparing projects for investment decisions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Through this competitive grant scheme, Norad aims to accelerate renewable energy investments in developing countries, particularly in regions where energy infrastructure gaps remain significant. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the Norad Renewable Energy Enterprise Development Grant The Norad Enterprise Development Scheme for Renewable Energy is a competitive grant program designed to promote private sector investments in renewable energy projects in developing countries. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The scheme aims to: Increase renewable energy generation capacity. Expand access to reliable electricity in underserved regions. Stimulate sustainable economic development. Reduce greenhouse gas emissions and promote climate mitigation. Encourage investment in renewable energy infrastructure. Many developing countries face major challenges in financing renewable energy projects due to high initial risks, insufficient infrastructure, and limited private sector involvement. This grant addresses those challenges by providing financial support during the project development phase, which is often the most uncertain stage for investors. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Through this initiative, Norad hopes to catalyze investments that will ultimately result in large-scale renewable energy installations and improved energy access. Objectives of the Grant Program The primary objective of the Norad Renewable Energy Grant is to support the development of commercially viable renewable energy projects in developing countries. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Specific objectives include: Supporting early-stage project development. Reducing financial risks associated with renewable energy investments. Improving energy infrastructure and grid connectivity. Encouraging private sector participation in clean energy markets. Accelerating the global energy transition toward renewable technologies. By addressing investment barriers and strengthening project preparation activities, the program helps transform innovative ideas into bankable energy projects. Types of Projects That Can Be Funded The Norad grant supports several types of activities that help renewable energy projects move toward investment and implementation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); These include: 1. Renewable Energy Project Development Studies The grant may support feasibility studies and development activities such as: Technical feasibility studies Environmental impact assessments Financial modeling and risk analysis Market and regulatory analysis Project structuring These activities help determine whether a renewable energy project is technically and financially viable. 2. Infrastructure Enabling Grid Connection Some renewable energy projects require additional infrastructure to connect to the national electricity grid. The grant may fund activities such as: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Access roads to project sites Grid interconnection facilities Transmission or distribution lines necessary for energy evacuation In cases where infrastructure is funded, ownership of the assets typically transfers to public authorities or utilities. 3. Guarantee Premiums for Debt Financing The grant may also support the cost of guarantee premiums required for long-term project financing. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); This support reduces the risk for lenders and investors, making it easier for developers to secure financing for renewable energy investments. Funding Amount and Cost Sharing The Norad Renewable Energy Enterprise Development Grant provides partial financial support. Key financial conditions include: Grants may cover up to 50 percent of eligible project costs. Applicants must provide at least 50 percent co-financing through their own funds or other financing sources. Payments are made after costs are incurred, meaning applicants must initially cover project expenses themselves before reimbursement. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); This co-financing requirement ensures that applicants have strong financial commitment and investment readiness. Who Can Apply for the Norad Renewable Energy Grant The program is primarily designed for commercial enterprises operating in the renewable energy sector. Eligible applicants include: Renewable energy developers Private companies working on renewable power projects Energy investment companies Project development companies ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Applications may also be submitted by consortia of eligible companies, provided each partner meets the eligibility criteria. However, some entities are not eligible to apply directly. Not Eligible The following organizations generally cannot apply directly: Non-governmental organizations (NGOs) Consulting companies without ownership in the project Equipment suppliers Entities without direct involvement in the renewable energy project. Applicants must demonstrate a significant stake in the project and active involvement in its implementation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet several technical, financial, and organizational criteria. Key eligibility requirements include: 1. Legal and Organizational Requirements Applicants must: Be an independent legal commercial entity Provide accurate documentation about their organization Demonstrate ownership or involvement in the project 2. Technical and Operational Capacity Applicants must show: Technical expertise in renewable energy development Administrative and operational capacity to manage projects Knowledge of the local context and energy sector in the project country 3. Financial Requirements Applicants must demonstrate financial strength through: Audited financial accounts from previous years Ability to finance at least 50 percent of project costs Capacity to fund activities before reimbursement In many cases, companies must show minimum turnover from commercial activities to qualify. 4. Governance and Ethical Compliance Applicants must also demonstrate strong governance frameworks including: Risk management systems Internal control mechanisms Anti-corruption policies Ethical guidelines addressing sexual exploitation, abuse, and harassment Compliance with international business standards such as the UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights. Countries Eligible for Project Implementation Projects must be implemented in developing countries eligible for Norwegian development assistance. Eligible regions include: Sub-Saharan Africa South Asia Southeast Asia Latin America Middle East and North Africa Small Island Developing States Examples of eligible countries include: Kenya Nigeria Tanzania Mozambique Nepal Bangladesh Ghana Uganda Pakistan Indonesia Norad places special priority on Sub-Saharan Africa and Least Developed Countries (LDCs). Priority countries may include: Malawi Mozambique Nepal Nigeria Somalia Tanzania Application Process The Norad Renewable Energy Grant uses a two-stage application process. Stage 1: Concept Note Submission Applicants must first submit: A concept note describing the project Audited financial accounts Confirmation of eligibility criteria The concept note provides an overview of the proposed project and allows Norad to assess its potential impact and feasibility. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Stage 2: Full Application Applicants who pass Stage 1 will be invited to submit a detailed proposal including: Full project documentation Financial models Implementation plans Additional supporting materials Only shortlisted applicants proceed to this stage. Apply here How to Apply Applications must be submitted through Norad s official grants portal. Applicants should carefully review: Eligibility requirements Application instructions Required documentation Evaluation criteria Evaluation and Selection Process Applications are evaluated based on multiple factors including: Technical feasibility of the project Financial viability Development impact Contribution to renewable energy access Alignment with Norad s priorities Applicant experience and capacity Because the program is highly competitive, meeting eligibility requirements does not guarantee funding. Norad typically signs 10 20 grant agreements per call, depending on available funding and application quality. Key Benefits of the Norad Renewable Energy Grant The grant provides several advantages for renewable energy companies: 1. Reduced Investment Risk Funding helps reduce financial risk during the early development phase. 2. Increased Access to Project Financing Support for guarantee premiums improves access to loans and investment capital. 3. Expansion into Emerging Markets Companies can expand renewable energy projects into high-growth developing markets. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(139); }); 4. Contribution to Climate Goals Projects contribute to global climate mitigation by increasing renewable energy capacity. Important Deadline Applicants must submit their Stage 1 concept note before: 24th March 2026 Late applications will not be accepted. The Norad Renewable Energy Grant 2026 represents a significant opportunity for commercial renewable energy developers and private companies seeking to expand energy projects in developing countries. By providing financial support during early project development, the program helps reduce investment risks, unlock financing opportunities, and accelerate renewable energy deployment in regions where access to electricity remains limited. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Norad Renewable Energy Grant 2026: Up to Millions in Funding for Private Companies Developing Renewable Energy Projects in Developing Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Iris Prize 2026 for Young Environmental Leaders Worldwide (Up to $15,000)
summary: The Iris Prize 2026 is an international funding opportunity designed to support young environmental leaders who are actively working to protect and restore nature within their communities. Organized by The Iris Project , this initiative provides financial awards and global recognition to youth-led projects that address environmental challenges and promote sustainable change. The prize exists to empower young people who are leading environmental solutions and to ensure that youth voices are recognized, supported, and funded in the global movement to address the climate and ecological crisis. Young innovators across the world who are developing creative ideas or managing impactful environmental projects can apply for this opportunity to receive funding, mentorship, and access to an international network of environmental leaders. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The Iris Prize specifically aims to address the funding gap faced by youth-led climate initiatives. Despite the growing global youth movement advocating for environmental action, only a small percentage of institutional climate funding reaches youth-led organizations. Through this prize, the organizers aim to change that reality by providing direct support to young environmental changemakers. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Overview of the Iris Prize Categories and Funding Amounts To ensure that young environmental initiatives at different stages of development receive appropriate support, the Iris Prize program offers three separate award categories. Each category targets projects at a specific stage, from early-stage ideas to established initiatives with the potential to scale globally. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); 1. Seed Prize Funding Amount: $5,000 Target Applicants: Young individuals or teams with innovative environmental ideas. Age Requirement: 14 24 years The Seed Prize is designed for applicants who have a groundbreaking idea to protect or restore nature but may not yet have fully implemented their project. This category supports early-stage environmental solutions that demonstrate creativity, community impact, and strong potential for future development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); 2. Stem Prize Funding Amount : $10,000 Target Applicants: Recently established environmental projects operating on a small scale. Age Requirement: 16 24 years The Stem Prize is intended for youth-led initiatives that have already begun operating and are starting to show measurable results. This category supports projects that need funding to strengthen their activities, expand their impact, or develop further. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); 3. Iris Prize Funding Amount: $15,000 Target Applicants: Established environmental projects with the ability to scale and replicate. Age Requirement: 16 24 years This is the highest-level award in the program. It is reserved for well-developed youth-led initiatives that are already demonstrating significant environmental impact and have the potential to expand to other communities or regions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Who Can Apply for the Iris Prize 2026? The Iris Prize is open to young environmental leaders from across the globe. The program welcomes applications from individuals, teams, or youth-led organizations working on environmental protection or restoration. Eligible Applicants Applicants must meet the following criteria: 1. Age Requirement Applicants must be between 14 and 24 years old depending on the prize category. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); 2. Youth-Led Projects The project must be led by young people, meaning that: Most of the leadership team must fall within the eligible age range. Young leaders must have real decision-making authority and autonomy within the project. 3. Environmental Focus Applicants must be working on projects that support: Protection of natural ecosystems Restoration of environmental resources Advocacy for environmental rights Community-based environmental solutions 4. Geographic Eligibility Applicants from all countries and continents are eligible to apply. However, the program prioritizes projects from the Global South, where environmental initiatives often face greater funding challenges and where support can have the most impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Types of Eligible Projects The Iris Prize accepts a wide range of environmental initiatives, including: New environmental ideas Small-scale projects Existing environmental programs with growth potential Community-based environmental restoration initiatives Youth environmental advocacy movements Innovative technological solutions addressing ecological challenges Projects associated with larger organizations, NGOs, or charities may also apply as long as young people maintain full leadership and decision-making control over the project. Additional Eligibility Requirements Applicants should also demonstrate the following values and principles: 1. Commitment to Environmental Protection Applicants must show that their project supports the protection and restoration of nature and promotes environmental sustainability. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); 2. Community-Based Impact The project should be locally driven, addressing environmental challenges identified by the community where it operates. 3. Diversity and Inclusion The program encourages initiatives that promote diversity, inclusion, and equitable participation in environmental action. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); 4. Safeguarding Requirements for Minors If applicants are minors according to their country s laws, they must partner with a local NGO or organization that will act as a fiscal sponsor. This organization will help manage funds and provide financial oversight for the project. Benefits of the Iris Prize The Iris Prize offers more than financial funding. Selected applicants gain access to a wide range of professional development opportunities and international networks. Key benefits include: 1. Financial Support Successful applicants receive grants ranging from $5,000 to $15,000, depending on the prize category. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); 2. Global Recognition Winning projects receive international exposure and recognition for their environmental impact. 3. Networking Opportunities Applicants are invited to join the Iris Project Ecosystem, a private collaboration platform where participants can connect with other environmental leaders, organizations, and funders. 4. Capacity Building and Mentorship Participants receive opportunities for training, collaboration, and knowledge-sharing with experts and fellow youth activists. 5. Documentary and Media Coverage Winning projects may be documented by local filmmakers and featured in the global awards ceremony. Iris Prize 2026 Application Timeline The selection process involves several stages designed to identify the most impactful youth-led environmental initiatives. March 4, 2026 Application portal opens. March April 2026 Applicants are invited to join the Iris Project collaboration ecosystem and networking platform. April 8, 2026 Final application deadline. May 2026 Shortlisted candidates participate in interviews and due diligence checks. June 2026 The expert judging panel reviews shortlisted projects. July 2026 Due diligence and funding verification for selected projects. August 2026 Winners and runners-up are notified. September 2026 Official announcement of winners during a Virtual Awards Ceremony. How to Apply for the Iris Prize 2026 The application process is designed to be accessible to young people from diverse backgrounds. Step 1: Complete the Eligibility Quiz Applicants must first complete an eligibility quiz available on the official application page. Step 2: Access the Application Form Once eligibility is confirmed, applicants gain access to the full application form. Step 3: Submit Project Details Applicants must provide information about their project, including: The environmental problem being addressed The proposed solution The role of young people in the project How the initiative promotes diversity and inclusion Step 4: Submit the Application Applications can be submitted online or offline and are accepted in any language, making the opportunity accessible to young leaders worldwide. Apply here Languages Accepted for the Application The Iris Prize application materials are available in several languages, including: English Spanish Portuguese French Arabic This multilingual approach ensures that youth from different linguistic backgrounds can apply without language barriers. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Why Young Environmental Leaders Should Apply The Iris Prize represents one of the few global funding opportunities designed specifically for youth-led environmental initiatives. With climate change and environmental degradation posing urgent global challenges, empowering young innovators has become a crucial part of developing sustainable solutions. By supporting youth leadership, the Iris Prize encourages innovative environmental thinking, strengthens grassroots environmental movements, and amplifies the voices of young changemakers worldwide. Application Deadline 8th April 2026 (23:59 CET) The Iris Prize 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for young environmental leaders to transform their ideas into impactful initiatives. Through financial grants, mentorship, and global exposure, the program helps young innovators expand their environmental solutions and inspire change within their communities. If you are a young environmental activist, innovator, or community leader with a passion for protecting nature, the Iris Prize could provide the funding and support needed to scale your impact. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Iris Prize 2026 for Young Environmental Leaders Worldwide (Up to $15,000) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Outdoor Facilitation: The Art of Facilitation in Nature 2026 in Nepal – 100% Scholarship Opportunity Available for ASEAN and SAARC Citizens
summary: Applications are now open for Outdoor Facilitation: The Art of Facilitation in Nature 2026 , an immersive eight-day professional development program designed to equip participants with essential facilitation, leadership, and outdoor education skills. The training will take place in a nature-rich environment near Kathmandu, Nepal, offering participants a unique opportunity to explore how learning, leadership, and personal development can unfold through nature-based experiences. Organized by YEP Academy, this program is designed for individuals who aspire to facilitate meaningful learning processes in outdoor environments. Participants will learn how to guide groups through experiential learning, design impactful outdoor educational programs, and build the core competencies required to lead groups safely and ethically in nature-based settings. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); In addition to professional training, the program offers 100% scholarships supported through donor subsidies from the German Government , specifically available for citizens of ASEAN and SAARC countries. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Program Overview Outdoor Facilitation: The Art of Facilitation in Nature serves as a foundational training for individuals who want to develop skills in outdoor facilitation and experiential education. The program combines theory, practical training, and reflective learning processes to help participants become confident facilitators capable of guiding individuals and groups through meaningful learning experiences in natural environments. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The program focuses on helping participants understand how people learn individually and in groups, how facilitators can support these processes, and how outdoor environments can be used as powerful learning spaces. Through experiential learning and reflective practice, participants will build confidence in their ability to design and facilitate safe, ethical, and impactful outdoor learning journeys. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Program Dates and Location The training will take place in Nepal in a nature-rich setting near Kathmandu using a basecamp model that allows participants to immerse themselves fully in the outdoor learning experience. Participants will meet at the training location on 19 April 2026 at 2:00 PM , giving everyone time to settle in and prepare their tents and equipment before the program officially begins at 5:00 PM. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); The course will run for eight days and seven nights , concluding on 26 April 2026 around 2:00 PM . Program Structure and Learning Experience The training is structured as an immersive residential experience that blends facilitation theory, outdoor skills, group work, and reflective practice. Each day follows a rhythm that allows participants to experience learning processes directly while also reflecting on them from the perspective of a facilitator. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Typical daily activities include morning check-ins that help participants connect with themselves and the group while setting intentions for the day. Nature-based experiential learning sessions form a central component of the program, enabling participants to explore leadership, communication, and group dynamics through direct engagement with the natural environment. Facilitation input sessions provide theoretical frameworks, tools, and models that help participants understand how learning processes unfold and how facilitators can guide them effectively. Participants also engage in guided group work and practice sessions where they apply facilitation skills and communication techniques in real learning scenarios. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Reflection and feedback sessions help deepen understanding and allow participants to practice giving and receiving constructive feedback. Evening circles and storytelling sessions provide space for participants to reflect on experiences, process emotions, and strengthen trust within the group. The program s flexible structure adapts to weather conditions, the surrounding environment, and the developmental stages of the group, allowing learning to emerge organically while simulating real conditions faced by outdoor facilitators. Key Learning Topics Participants will explore several important topics related to facilitation, leadership, and experiential learning. The program covers how people learn across different life stages, including children, adolescents, and adults, and what these learning processes mean for outdoor education. Participants will also examine the ethical responsibilities and roles of facilitators, distinguishing between facilitation, training, and coaching. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Core facilitation skills are emphasized, including session structure, guiding group processes, asking powerful questions, and creating spaces where participants feel safe and supported. Communication training includes active listening, nonviolent communication, feedback culture, and conflict awareness. Participants will learn how to recognize and respond to emotional dynamics within groups while maintaining respectful and constructive communication. Group development processes are explored to help participants understand how groups evolve and how facilitation approaches must adapt accordingly. The program also introduces basic conflict awareness and de-escalation techniques. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Participants will develop awareness of outdoor safety principles such as risk perception, safety mindset, and basic field routines that help ensure safe group experiences in nature. The training also emphasizes facilitator self-care and self-management practices, including grounding techniques, emotional resilience, and maintaining personal boundaries when leading groups. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Outdoor Skills and Facilitation Competencies Participants will develop practical outdoor facilitation competencies, including camp management, risk awareness, and first aid considerations in outdoor environments. They will learn how to design and implement outdoor learning programs, prepare facilitation agendas, and co-facilitate learning sessions effectively. The program also helps participants develop the confidence to navigate group dynamics, guide reflective learning processes, and evaluate educational experiences with professionalism and presence. Facilitators The program is led by experienced practitioners in outdoor education and facilitation. The lead facilitator is Dirk Reber, a practitioner specializing in ecotherapy, outdoor education, and process facilitation. He has decades of experience designing and leading transformative nature-based learning programs across diverse ecological and cultural contexts. He oversees curriculum design, program leadership, safety coordination, supervision, and participant feedback. Full bio and links will be available on the Team Page . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); The co-facilitator is Anu Rai, a seasoned trekker and educator with expertise in transforming outdoor adventures into transformative learning experiences. Her work focuses on sustainable trekking, environmental awareness, and experiential education in mountain and forest environments. Full bio and links will be available on the Team Page . Participant Group Size To ensure a safe and meaningful learning environment, the program maintains a small participant group size. A minimum of six and a maximum of twelve participants will be accepted for the training. This small cohort allows facilitators to provide individualized support, foster deeper interaction among participants, and create a strong sense of community throughout the learning journey. Certification Participants who successfully complete the program will receive a Certificate of Continuing Professional Development (CPD) . To qualify for certification, participants must attend all sessions and actively engage in group processes, reflective activities, and facilitation practice. Participants who wish to pursue further professional certification can continue their training through additional programs offered by YEP Academy. Completion of further intensive training journeys and a mentored facilitation practice phase may lead to certification as a Certified Outdoor Youth Facilitator . Participants who complete the full training pathway and develop their own outdoor learning program may receive the designation International Nature and Outdoor Youth Facilitator and Leader . Safety and Risk Management Participant safety and well-being are central to the program design. Comprehensive risk management procedures are implemented to ensure a safe learning environment. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Participants provide emergency contact and medical information during registration. Signed consent forms confirm understanding of safety procedures before the program begins. All facilitators are certified in first aid, and accident insurance coverage of up to USD 5,000 is provided for each participant. Location-specific risk assessments are conducted prior to the program to identify and mitigate potential hazards. The campsite environment is designed to provide a stable and secure basecamp where participants can focus on learning without exposure to high-risk adventure activities. Program Fee and What Is Included The program fee covers all facilitation sessions and training materials provided throughout the program. Accommodation and campsite fees are included as part of the basecamp learning model. Accident insurance coverage is also included. Food arrangements include daily lunches prepared and served during the program, as well as group-prepared dinners where ingredients are provided and participants cook together. Breakfast is organized independently by participants. Participants will also attend a preparation meeting held online two weeks prior to the program. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(139); }); What Is Not Included Participants are responsible for arranging travel to and from the meeting location in Nepal. Visa fees, vaccinations, and personal outdoor equipment such as tents, sleeping bags, clothing, and daypacks are not included in the program fee. Accommodation before or after the training is also not included unless otherwise specified. Optional equipment rental may be available through YEP Academy for tents, sleeping bags, and sleeping mats for an additional fee. Scholarships The program offers 100 percent scholarships funded through donor subsidies from the German Government . These scholarships are available exclusively for citizens of ASEAN and SAARC countries . Applicants who wish to apply for the scholarship must download the scholarship application form and submit it by email to info@yep-academy.org . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(140); }); Scholarship Deadline The deadline to apply for the scholarship is 5 April 2026 . Standard Ticket Registration Participants who do not apply for the scholarship may register for the program through the standard ticket option. The standard ticket costs USD 890 , and ticket sales close on 14 April 2026 at 11:50 PM . Where can I find important downloads? Download to Know More What to Pack: Packing List Download to Get to Know the Dos and Don ts: Code of Conduct Download to Be Aware of Our Policies: Terms and Conditions ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(141); }); Contact Information For further information about the program, applicants may contact YEP Academy via email at info@yep-academy.org . Read more and apply for the Outdoor Facilitation: The Art of Facilitation in Nature 2026 HERE . Click HERE to explore more global opportunities on OFY.
