title: UNESCO 2026 Global Opportunity: Apply for the “Strengthening Lifelong Learning Systems through Multi-Level and Multi-Sectoral Engagement” Programme (Fully Supported Policy Development Initiative
summary: The UNESCO Institute for Lifelong Learning (UIL), in collaboration with Shanghai Open University , has announced a new international programme aimed at strengthening lifelong learning systems worldwide. This programme forms part of UNESCO s broader commitment to supporting governments in developing inclusive, flexible and sustainable learning systems that provide education opportunities throughout a person s life. By encouraging cooperation across multiple sectors and levels of governance, the initiative aims to create stronger learning ecosystems capable of responding to the social, economic and technological challenges of the 21st century. Introduction The UNESCO Institute for Lifelong Learning (UIL) has officially announced a global call for applications for its 2026 programme titled Strengthening Lifelong Learning Systems through Multi-Level and Multi-Sectoral Engagement. This initiative is implemented in partnership with Shanghai Open University (SOU) and is designed to support countries in building stronger and more integrated lifelong learning systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The programme represents the first phase of a three-year international initiative aimed at strengthening national education systems by integrating lifelong learning into governance, policy frameworks, and institutional coordination across sectors. Participating countries will receive technical support, policy guidance, and international collaboration opportunities to advance their lifelong learning strategies and improve education systems at national and local levels. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); This opportunity is particularly important in the context of global education reforms aligned with Sustainable Development Goal 4 (SDG 4), which calls for inclusive, equitable quality education and lifelong learning opportunities for all. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Overview of the UNESCO Lifelong Learning Systems Programme The Strengthening Lifelong Learning Systems through Multi-Level and Multi-Sectoral Engagement programme focuses on helping countries design, implement, and strengthen policies that support learning throughout life. The programme recognizes that education systems must go beyond traditional schooling to address the needs of adults, workers, communities, and lifelong learners. By fostering collaboration across governments, institutions, and sectors, the initiative aims to create coherent learning ecosystems that support individuals throughout their lives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Countries selected for the 2026 cohort will participate in a structured policy development and capacity-building process supported by global experts in education governance, lifelong learning, and policy implementation. Objectives of the Programme The programme seeks to help participating countries achieve several strategic objectives related to lifelong learning systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); 1. Strengthening Governance and Policy Coordination One of the main objectives of the programme is to improve the governance structures that oversee lifelong learning policies. Many countries have fragmented education policies that separate adult education, vocational training, and formal schooling. The programme helps governments integrate these components into a coherent lifelong learning system. 2. Improving Alignment Between National and Local Policies Countries often face challenges in translating national education policies into effective local implementation. Through this programme, countries will work to ensure that national strategies align with implementation at regional and city levels, including learning cities and local education ecosystems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); 3. Developing Policy Instruments for Lifelong Learning Another major objective is to help governments develop or refine policy tools such as: National lifelong learning strategies Action plans for adult education Education reform roadmaps Skills development frameworks These policy instruments help countries create sustainable structures for learning across all stages of life. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); 4. Building National Capacity Participating countries will receive support to build national capacity for managing lifelong learning as a cross-sectoral policy area involving education, labor markets, community institutions, and civil society. Benefits of the Programme Countries selected for this initiative will gain access to a wide range of benefits designed to support policy reform and knowledge exchange. 1. Tailored Technical Assistance Participating countries will receive direct technical support from experts affiliated with the UNESCO Institute for Lifelong Learning and partner institutions. This assistance will help countries identify policy gaps and implement effective reforms. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); 2. Online Learning Modules and Webinars Participants will take part in curated online training modules and international webinars focusing on: Lifelong learning systems Education governance policy implementation cross-sector collaboration capacity building These sessions allow national stakeholders to develop new expertise in designing lifelong learning frameworks. 3. National Lifelong Learning System Profile Each participating country will conduct a contextual analysis of its current lifelong learning ecosystem. The analysis will lead to the creation of a national profile describing: policy frameworks institutional arrangements education infrastructure governance structures future reform priorities 4. Participation in the GNLC Academy Campus Countries may also participate in activities connected to the Global Network of Learning Cities (GNLC). This engagement allows policymakers to collaborate with cities and governments worldwide that are actively developing lifelong learning ecosystems. 5. Policy Dialogue Events Selected countries will receive support to organize a national or sub-regional policy dialogue event during 2026. These dialogues bring together stakeholders such as: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); government ministries researchers education institutions civil society organizations development partners The discussions help translate programme outcomes into concrete policy changes. 6. Follow-Up Support After policy dialogue events, UNESCO experts will provide follow-up assistance to help integrate the results into national policies and reforms. Category of Applicants Eligible for the Programme Unlike individual scholarships or fellowships, this programme is designed for national government institutions. Eligible Applicants Applications must be submitted by: National ministries responsible for lifelong learning Ministries of education or equivalent government bodies National agencies responsible for adult education or education policy ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Applications must be coordinated with relevant national stakeholders, including local governments and education institutions. Countries Eligible to Apply The programme is open to UNESCO Member States worldwide. This means that countries from all regions and continents may apply, including: Africa Asia Europe North America South America Oceania Middle East The programme seeks to include a small and diverse cohort of countries to ensure meaningful collaboration and peer learning. Key Requirements for Participating Countries Countries selected for the programme must demonstrate strong institutional commitment. 1. Official Government Engagement Applications must be submitted by a national ministry or relevant government authority responsible for lifelong learning policy. 2. National Leadership Team Each participating country must designate a national leadership team that will coordinate programme activities. 3. Stakeholder Coalition Countries must involve stakeholders from different sectors, including: education authorities local governments civil society organizations learning cities research institutions 4. Resource Mobilization Participating countries must mobilize the necessary human and financial resources required to implement programme activities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); 5. Policy Dialogue Organization Each country must organize at least one national or sub-regional policy dialogue event during the programme. 6. Policy Integration Countries are expected to integrate programme results into public policies and national education strategies. Programme Timeline The initiative begins in 2026 and forms part of a three-year policy development programme. Key stages include: Selection of participating countries Orientation and programme onboarding Online learning modules and webinars National contextual analysis Policy dialogue events Follow-up policy implementation support Why Lifelong Learning Systems Matter Education is increasingly recognized as a lifelong process that continues beyond traditional schooling. Rapid technological change, evolving labor markets, and global challenges such as climate change require individuals to continuously update their knowledge and skills. Lifelong learning policies support: adult education and literacy workforce reskilling and upskilling social inclusion community development sustainable economic growth International organizations such as UNESCO view lifelong learning systems as a critical foundation for inclusive and sustainable societies. Application Process Interested governments must complete the official application form provided by the UNESCO Institute for Lifelong Learning. Apply here Applications should outline: national priorities for lifelong learning current policy frameworks stakeholders involved commitment to programme participation After selection, participating countries will formalize their involvement through a letter of intent issued by the responsible ministry. Application Deadline 20th March 2026 The UNESCO programme on Strengthening Lifelong Learning Systems through Multi-Level and Multi-Sectoral Engagement offers a unique opportunity for governments to improve their education policies and develop integrated lifelong learning frameworks. Through international collaboration, technical expertise, and policy dialogue, participating countries will gain the tools needed to build inclusive, flexible, and resilient education systems capable of supporting learning throughout life. For countries committed to advancing education reform and achieving Sustainable Development Goal 4, this initiative represents a strategic opportunity to strengthen national education ecosystems and create sustainable lifelong learning pathways for their citizens. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post UNESCO 2026 Global Opportunity: Apply for the “Strengthening Lifelong Learning Systems through Multi-Level and Multi-Sectoral Engagement” Programme (Fully Supported Policy Development Initiative appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Global Research Funding: €12M Horizon Europe Call on Sustainable Agriculture and Food Systems
summary: Researchers and institutions working on sustainable agriculture now have an exciting opportunity to secure major European research funding. The European Commission has launched a new call under Horizon Europe Cluster 6 , inviting proposals that focus on innovative solutions for sustainable and climate-smart agriculture systems. The call HORIZON-CL6-2027-02-FARM2FORK-02 (two-stage) supports research projects aimed at optimising the water-nutrient-energy nexus to strengthen agricultural sustainability and improve food system resilience. The initiative aligns with the European Union Farm to Fork Strategy , which seeks to transform food systems to become fair, healthy, and environmentally friendly. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the Funding Opportunity This Horizon Europe call will fund collaborative research projects that advance sustainable food production systems while improving resource efficiency across agriculture. Projects are expected to generate innovative technologies, strategies, or practices that can strengthen climate-smart agriculture and enhance food security. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Successful proposals should demonstrate strong scientific excellence , innovation potential , and clear impact on sustainable and resilient food systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Funding Details Total indicative budget: 12,000,000 Estimated EU contribution per project: Around 6,000,000 Funding type: Horizon Europe Innovation Action (Lump-Sum Grant) Call reference: HORIZON-CL6-2027-02-FARM2FORK-02 (two-stage) Focus Areas Projects funded under this call are expected to: Develop innovative solutions that optimise the water nutrient energy nexus in agriculture Support climate-smart and sustainable agricultural systems Improve resource efficiency in food production Contribute to resilient and sustainable food systems aligned with the EU Farm-to-Fork strategy Strengthen research and innovation that enhances the sustainability and competitiveness of agricultural systems ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Eligibility Criteria The call is open to a wide range of stakeholders including: Research institutions Universities Private companies Non-governmental organizations (NGOs) Multi-stakeholder research consortia Applicants must submit proposals as international research consortia , typically involving organizations from at least three different EU Member States or Horizon Europe Associated Countries . Geographic Scope The call primarily targets organizations based in: European Union Member States Horizon Europe Associated Countries However, international partners including organizations from Africa and other regions can participate as members of eligible research consortia , making this a valuable opportunity for African research institutions seeking international collaboration. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Important Dates Call opening date: 3 February 2027 First-stage proposal deadline: 7 April 2027 How to Apply Interested applicants can access the application portal and detailed call information here: Apply Here Stay Updated To receive verified updates on funding opportunities, partnerships, fellowships, and global programs for Africans, follow Global Grants and Opportunities for Africa (GGA) : Discover more Grant opportunities Here. The post Global Research Funding: €12M Horizon Europe Call on Sustainable Agriculture and Food Systems appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: TOCA Online Virtual Language Fellowship: Study Arabic, French, or Spanish with Global Peers Through Cultural Exchange
summary: Language learning is no longer limited to classrooms or textbooks. With the increasing importance of global communication and cultural understanding, innovative programs are emerging to connect students worldwide. One such initiative is TOCA Online , a virtual language learning and cultural exchange program that provides students with the opportunity to build language skills while connecting with diverse communities across the globe. Implemented by Teens of Color Abroad (TOCA) and supported by the J. Christopher Stevens Virtual Exchange Initiative (JCSVEI) under the U.S. Department of State s Bureau of Educational and Cultural Affairs, TOCA Online aims to create meaningful international connections through virtual exchange. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The program is designed for high school and university students who want to improve their language proficiency, develop intercultural communication skills, and engage in global conversations with peers and refugee educators worldwide. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); What is TOCA Online? TOCA Online is a virtual language and cultural exchange program that allows students to learn languages such as Arabic, French, and Spanish from native speakers. The program places a strong emphasis on dialogue, cultural awareness, and global collaboration. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Students are taught by refugee language partners who are native speakers of the language they teach. These instructors are located in different parts of the world and bring valuable cultural perspectives into each lesson. Through this approach, students gain both linguistic competence and cultural understanding. The program also integrates workshops, peer exchanges, and collaborative projects designed to encourage cross-cultural dialogue and global awareness among participants. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Program Structure TOCA Online offers two main program tracks: High School Virtual Language Program The high school program is designed for students between the ages of 13 and 18 and runs for three weeks . During this period, students participate in virtual language instruction sessions combined with cultural exchange activities. Participants can choose from several language options depending on their interests and goals. Available courses include: Introduction to Arabic (20 hours) Introduction to French (10 hours) Introduction to Spanish (10 hours) Advanced Spanish (10 hours) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Students take part in live online sessions where they practice speaking and listening skills while interacting with peers from around the world. In addition to language classes, students attend Refugee Voices workshops , which feature distinguished refugee speakers who share their stories and experiences. These sessions provide students with insights into global migration, resilience, and cultural diversity. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); College-Level Arabic Language Exchange Program TOCA Online also offers a college-level program focused on Arabic language practice and cross-cultural collaboration. This program connects students from Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs) in the United States with university students from the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Participants engage in: One-on-one Arabic and English language exchanges Interactive online modules Cultural workshops Collaborative virtual sessions The exchange typically runs for seven to eight weeks , allowing participants to build meaningful connections while improving their language skills. At the end of the program, American participants submit short video introductions in Arabic demonstrating their newly acquired skills, while MENA participants present their experiences collaborating with U.S. students and discuss how the program helped prepare them for the workforce. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Currently, the college-level opportunity is available only to students from partnering HBCUs . Program Options and Costs TOCA Online provides two participation options for high school students. Standard Program The standard program is offered free of charge , making it accessible to students from various backgrounds. Participants in the standard program receive: Live virtual language instruction Refugee Voices workshops Cultural exchange sessions A digital certificate of completion Access to alumni opportunities and networking resources Immersive Program Students seeking a more intensive experience can enroll in the Immersive Program , which costs USD 250 . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); This option includes all benefits of the standard program plus additional features such as: Five hours of personalized one-on-one language practice A TOCA completion package containing a t-shirt, pen, sticker, printed certificate, and activity book Invitations to three virtual culinary lessons The immersive option allows participants to deepen their language practice and gain a richer cultural experience. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Key Benefits of Participating in TOCA Online Participating in TOCA Online offers several valuable benefits for students interested in international education and cross-cultural communication. Development of Language Skills Students significantly improve their speaking and listening skills by interacting directly with native speakers through live sessions. Cultural Exchange and Global Awareness The program fosters meaningful cultural exchange by connecting students with peers and educators from diverse backgrounds worldwide. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Networking and Global Connections Participants build friendships and professional connections with other globally minded students and educators. Access to Future Opportunities Students receive access to scholarships, educational opportunities, and international programs through TOCA s alumni network and newsletters. Certificates and Recommendations Participants earn a certificate of completion and may qualify for letters of recommendation to support future academic and career pursuits. Why Virtual Exchange Matters Virtual exchange programs like TOCA Online are becoming increasingly important in the modern educational landscape. They allow students to connect with people across borders, develop intercultural competence, and gain real-world communication skills. These experiences prepare young people for global careers in fields such as international relations, diplomacy, global business, education, and humanitarian work. By engaging with students and educators worldwide, participants develop empathy, cultural understanding, and the ability to collaborate across cultural and geographic boundaries. Testimonials from Participants Students, educators, and parents consistently praise the impact of the TOCA Online program. One educator noted that the program complements classroom learning by reinforcing language skills in an interactive environment. Parents have also highlighted the program s impact on their children s academic and career paths. Some participants have gone on to pursue language studies at the university level after completing the program. Many students report gaining confidence in speaking their target language and developing meaningful international friendships through the program. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Application Timeline The application cycle for the 2025 TOCA Online program has closed . Students interested in participating in the future are encouraged to join the program s newsletter to receive updates when applications open for the 2026 season . Applicants may be invited to participate in a virtual interview with TOCA staff to discuss their interest and commitment to the program. Prospective participants are encouraged to monitor announcements to ensure they do not miss the next application cycle. for more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post TOCA Online Virtual Language Fellowship: Study Arabic, French, or Spanish with Global Peers Through Cultural Exchange appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships 2026: Fully Funded Government Scholarship for International Students to Study in New Zealand
summary: The Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships programme is one of the most prestigious international education opportunities funded by the Government of New Zealand. Designed to support sustainable development and strengthen global partnerships, the scholarship offers fully funded opportunities for students from eligible developing countries to pursue higher education in New Zealand. Administered through the New Zealand Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade under the New Zealand Aid Programme and supported by institutions such as Education New Zealand and Massey University, the Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship aims to empower talented individuals with the knowledge, leadership skills, and professional expertise needed to contribute to the social and economic development of their home countries. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); This scholarship is highly competitive and provides comprehensive funding for international students to pursue undergraduate and postgraduate studies in New Zealand universities and institutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Purpose of the Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships The Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships were created to strengthen international relationships and support global development by investing in education. The scholarship programme focuses on building leadership capacity among students from developing countries. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Through this initiative, scholars gain world-class education in New Zealand while developing skills and expertise that can contribute to the long-term development of their home nations. The programme aims to: Improve professional and technical skills among international scholars Promote sustainable development in partner countries Strengthen diplomatic and educational ties between New Zealand and developing nations Provide students with the knowledge required to address economic, social, and environmental challenges in their countries ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Scholars are expected to return home after completing their studies to apply their knowledge and contribute to their nation s development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Scholarship Benefits and Funding Coverage The Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship is a fully funded opportunity, meaning selected scholars receive comprehensive financial support for the duration of their studies. Benefits may include: 1. Full Tuition Fees The scholarship covers the full cost of tuition at approved universities or institutes in New Zealand. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); 2. Living Allowance Scholars receive a regular stipend to cover daily living expenses such as accommodation, food, and personal costs. 3. International Travel The scholarship covers round-trip airfare from the scholar s home country to New Zealand at the start and end of the scholarship. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); 4. Travel Insurance Recipients are provided with medical and travel insurance during their scholarship period. 5. Home Leave Travel Students studying longer than 1.6 years may be eligible for one or two trips home during the scholarship period, depending on the program duration and border regulations. 6. Research and Thesis Support Postgraduate scholars may receive financial support for thesis preparation and research-related costs. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); 7. Tutoring Assistance Certain undergraduate programmes may receive additional tutoring support of up to NZ$1000 per year. 8. Reintegration Allowance After completing the scholarship, scholars receive a NZ$1000 reintegration allowance to help them settle back into their home country. Eligible Fields of Study The scholarship supports a wide range of academic disciplines. However, the New Zealand Aid Programme prioritizes fields that directly support the development needs of participating countries. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Common priority areas include: Agriculture and environmental science Climate change and sustainability Engineering and infrastructure Public health and medical sciences Education and teacher training Business and economic development Governance and public policy Disaster risk management Applicants are encouraged to select courses aligned with the development priorities of their home countries. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Category of People Eligible to Apply The Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship targets individuals who demonstrate leadership potential and commitment to contributing to the development of their home country. Eligible categories include: Undergraduate students seeking a bachelor s degree Graduates applying for master s or postgraduate degrees Professionals seeking advanced academic training Government officials or development professionals Community leaders and researchers Applicants must show a strong commitment to returning to their country after completing their studies. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Countries and Regions Eligible for the Scholarship The Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship is available to citizens of specific eligible countries, mainly from the Pacific and parts of Asia. Eligible regions typically include: Pacific Countries Examples include: Fiji Samoa Tonga Solomon Islands Kiribati Tuvalu Vanuatu Niue ASEAN and Selected Asian Countries Certain countries in Southeast and South Asia may also qualify. Applicants must confirm that their country is listed among the official eligible countries before applying. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet a number of eligibility criteria before applying for the scholarship: 1. Age Requirement Applicants must be at least 18 years old when the scholarship begins. 2. Citizenship Applicants must hold citizenship from one of the eligible countries listed in the scholarship programme. 3. Residency Requirement Applicants are generally required to have lived in their home country for a certain period before applying. 4. Academic Requirements Applicants must meet admission requirements for their chosen university and programme in New Zealand. 5. Commitment to Home Country Development Applicants must demonstrate their willingness to return home after completing their studies and contribute to national development. 6. Work Experience Some programmes, particularly postgraduate ones, may require relevant professional experience. 7. Scholarship History Applicants who have previously received a Manaaki scholarship may not be eligible to apply again. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Study Mode and Location Scholarship recipients must study full-time and on campus in New Zealand. Students may enroll in one of New Zealand s universities or institutes of technology offering eligible programmes. Application Process The application process for the Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship involves several stages. Step 1: Check Eligibility Applicants must complete the scholarship eligibility questionnaire to determine whether they qualify for the programme. Step 2: Research Courses Applicants should identify suitable courses offered by New Zealand institutions that align with their academic background and career goals. Step 3: Register Online Eligible applicants must create an account on the Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship online portal. Step 4: Submit Application Applicants must complete and submit their application before the deadline. Step 5: Selection Process Applications are assessed based on: Academic merit Leadership potential Development impact Commitment to contributing to the applicant s home country Apply here Selection Criteria The selection committee considers several factors when evaluating applications, including: Academic excellence Professional achievements Leadership potential Development impact of proposed study Alignment with national development priorities Applicants who perform well during the evaluation process may be shortlisted and invited for interviews. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(139); }); Important Deadline Applicants must submit their application before the official deadline. Application Deadline: 31st March 2026 Late applications are not accepted, and the application portal may close early if the maximum number of submissions is reached. Why the Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship Is Highly Competitive The Manaaki scholarship is considered one of the most prestigious international scholarships for students from developing nations. Its comprehensive funding package, high-quality education opportunities, and focus on leadership development make it extremely competitive. Thousands of applicants from around the world compete each year for a limited number of scholarships. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(140); }); Successful candidates usually demonstrate: Strong academic records Leadership potential Clear development goals Commitment to returning home after graduation The Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships represent a life-changing opportunity for students from eligible developing countries who wish to pursue world-class education in New Zealand. By providing full financial support and access to top universities, the programme empowers scholars to gain advanced knowledge, develop leadership skills, and contribute meaningfully to the growth of their nations. For individuals who aspire to study abroad while making a lasting impact in their communities, the Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship is an opportunity worth pursuing. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships 2026: Fully Funded Government Scholarship for International Students to Study in New Zealand appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Institute for Youth in Policy Public Policy Fellowship 2026: Global Opportunity for Young Leaders to Develop Real-World Policy Solutions
summary: The Institute for Youth in Policy (YIP) Public Policy Fellowship is a highly competitive international leadership and policy development program designed for emerging young leaders interested in shaping the future of public policy. This intensive two-month fellowship empowers participants to collaborate, conduct policy analysis, and propose innovative cross-sector solutions addressing critical global challenges in social, political, economic, and environmental systems. With an acceptance rate averaging 8 10%, the fellowship is one of the most selective youth policy development opportunities available. It brings together talented students, researchers, activists, and future policymakers from around the world to learn directly from experienced mentors, policy professionals, and academic leaders. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Participants gain hands-on experience in policy research, project management, communication, and teamwork while developing a comprehensive capstone project designed to influence real-world policy discussions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the Institute for Youth in Policy Fellowship The Public Policy Fellowship is a flagship program of the Institute for Youth in Policy that focuses on cultivating the next generation of policy thinkers and civic leaders. The fellowship is built around collaborative learning, mentorship, and practical policy development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The program lasts two months and provides a structured environment where fellows examine public policy through local, national, and global perspectives. Fellows work closely with peers and policy professionals to evaluate current policy issues and design actionable solutions. The fellowship emphasizes interdisciplinary thinking and encourages participants to explore diverse sectors such as: Social policy and civic engagement Economic development and governance Environmental sustainability Urban development and renewal Technology policy and smart infrastructure Education reform Public sector innovation ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); By the end of the program, fellows will have produced a policy-focused capstone project and gained practical skills that help them engage meaningfully with public policy processes. Fellowship Impact and Achievements The Institute for Youth in Policy fellowship has already demonstrated significant global impact through the work of its participants. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Program achievements include: 1000+ fellows have graduated from the fellowship program 300+ policy capstone projects have been published 3.2 million views recorded on fellows published policy capstones 80% of YIP leadership members originally participated in the fellowship 8 10% acceptance rate, highlighting the program s competitive selection process These numbers demonstrate the program s growing reputation as a leading youth policy initiative. Fellowship Program Structure The fellowship is structured to combine education, mentorship, collaboration, and practical policy development. During the two-month program, fellows will participate in: 1. Policy Workshops and Learning Sessions Participants attend workshops led by policy experts, academics, and experienced practitioners. These sessions introduce fellows to real policy frameworks and analytical tools used in policymaking. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); 2. Collaborative Research Projects Fellows work in teams with peers from diverse backgrounds to research and develop policy ideas. Collaboration is a core element of the program, allowing fellows to explore different perspectives. 3. Mentorship and Networking Participants engage with YIP department executives, policy experts, and civic leaders who provide guidance throughout the program. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); 4. Policy Analysis and Project Development Fellows conduct research on key issues and develop actionable policy recommendations. 5. Capstone Project Each fellow completes a policy capstone project, which is published and shared with the wider policy community. Capstone projects often focus on: Urban policy and city development Climate and environmental policy Education systems reform Digital governance and technology Social equity initiatives Economic policy innovation ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); These projects represent the culmination of the fellow s research and collaboration throughout the program. What Fellows Gain from the Program The fellowship is designed to provide both academic insight and practical policy experience. Participants benefit from: 1. Policy Development Skills Fellows learn how policies are designed, analyzed, and implemented in real-world governance structures. 2. Leadership Development Participants build leadership abilities through teamwork, collaborative decision-making, and project management. 3. Research and Analytical Skills The fellowship teaches structured approaches to policy analysis and data-driven problem solving. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); 4. Communication and Advocacy Skills Fellows develop the ability to communicate policy ideas clearly through writing, presentations, and public engagement. 5. Professional Networking Participants build connections with policymakers, educators, and civic leaders that can support future careers. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Fellowship Cohort Dates for 2026 The Institute for Youth in Policy offers multiple fellowship sessions each year. Winter Cohort 17th January 2026 21st March 2026 Spring Cohort 11th April 2026 6th June 2026 Summer Cohort 20th June 2026 22nd August 2026 Fall Cohort 12th September 2026 7th November 2026 Applicants should apply before the relevant cohort deadline for the Spring 2026 cohort and decisions will be announced on 1st April 2026. Geographic Eligibility The Institute for Youth in Policy fellowship is open to applicants from around the world. Eligible applicants may come from: Africa Asia Europe North America South America Oceania This global participation helps ensure diverse perspectives and collaborative policy discussions. What Happens After the Fellowship Graduates of the fellowship often continue their policy work in various ways. Many fellows go on to: Join leadership roles within the Institute for Youth in Policy Publish policy research and commentary Participate in civic initiatives and policy advocacy Pursue careers in government, law, international development, or public service Launch policy initiatives within their communities Because fellows publish their capstone projects, participants leave the program with tangible work that can strengthen academic or professional portfolios. Who Can Apply The fellowship is designed for young emerging leaders who are passionate about public policy and civic engagement. Eligible Categories of Applicants The program welcomes applications from individuals such as: High school students University students Recent graduates Youth activists Aspiring policymakers Researchers interested in governance Civic engagement advocates Applicants should demonstrate a strong interest in social impact and public policy. How to Apply Interested applicants must submit their applications before the relevant deadline through the official application form on the Institute for Youth in Policy website. Applicants should prepare to provide information about their interests in public policy, leadership experiences, and motivations for joining the fellowship. Due to the highly selective nature of the program, applicants are encouraged to submit thoughtful and well-prepared applications. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Apply here Application Deadline: 27th March 2026 (Spring Cohort) The Institute for Youth in Policy fellowship represents a powerful platform for youth engagement in policymaking. title: WWF BRIDGE Paid Summer Internship 2026: Fully Paid Global Internship Opportunity for Students Interested in Conservation Careers
summary: Students and young professionals seeking a meaningful career in environmental protection and sustainability now have a major opportunity through the World Wildlife Fund (WWF). The WWF BRIDGE Internship Program offers a highly competitive paid summer internship designed to introduce undergraduate and graduate students to the conservation sector while providing practical professional experience. The BRIDGE program has become one of the most recognized conservation internships globally, giving students exposure to real-world environmental work, mentorship from leading experts, and training designed to prepare them for careers in sustainability, climate action, wildlife protection, and environmental policy. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the WWF BRIDGE Internship Program The WWF BRIDGE Internship Program is a paid internship initiative launched in 2021 to support students interested in careers related to environmental conservation, sustainability, biodiversity protection, and climate change solutions. The program was designed to create pathways for talented students who may not previously have had professional exposure to conservation careers. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); The goal of the program is to connect the next generation of leaders with opportunities that combine professional development, mentorship, and hands-on project work within WWF s global conservation initiatives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The BRIDGE Internship seeks to: Increase exposure of students to careers in conservation and sustainability Provide practical work experience in the environmental sector Reduce barriers that prevent students from accessing professional internships Create a pipeline of future conservation professionals and leaders Through this initiative, interns work directly with WWF departments on research, communications, policy, climate programs, environmental strategy, and more. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Since its launch, the program has attracted thousands of applicants and has produced hundreds of alumni who are now working in environmental careers across the globe. Internship Benefits and Salary One of the major advantages of the WWF BRIDGE Internship is that it is fully paid, ensuring that students can participate without financial barriers. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Internship Pay Undergraduate interns: $20.50 per hour Graduate interns: $22.50 per hour The internship typically runs 35 hours per week for approximately 10 weeks. Additional Benefits Participants in the BRIDGE Internship also gain access to several professional and workplace benefits, including: Professional training programs Resume writing and interview preparation workshops Salary negotiation training Networking opportunities with WWF leadership Access to the eCornell professional development program Exposure to WWF global conservation projects Mentorship and career guidance Flexible work arrangements including virtual and hybrid positions ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Interns also benefit from workplace amenities such as: Compressed work schedules with every other Friday off Paid sick leave for certain locations Access to employee assistance resources Forest conservation research Biodiversity protection Climate change mitigation projects Natural resource management Marine conservation Professional networking events with WWF staff and experts. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Fields and Internship Areas Interns in the BRIDGE program can work across various WWF departments depending on available positions. Examples of previous internship roles include: Conservation and Environmental Programs Forest conservation research Biodiversity protection Climate change mitigation projects Natural resource management Marine conservation Research and Policy Environmental policy research Sustainable development strategies Climate risk analysis Impact investing and sustainability markets ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Communications and Marketing Environmental awareness campaigns Social media strategies for conservation Public outreach initiatives Development and Partnerships Grant proposal research Environmental funding initiatives Corporate sustainability partnerships Program Operations Sustainable procurement strategies Institutional sustainability planning These roles allow interns to gain direct experience working on global environmental challenges. Countries and Regions Eligible The WWF BRIDGE Internship is open internationally , meaning students from all countries and continents may apply depending on the specific internship role and location. Eligible applicants can come from regions including: Africa Asia Europe North America South America Oceania However, some internship roles may have specific location or work authorization requirements, particularly for positions based in the United States or hybrid roles. Required Qualifications While the program welcomes applicants from diverse academic backgrounds, strong candidates usually demonstrate: Academic Qualifications Applicants should be studying fields such as: Environmental science Wildlife conservation Ecology Sustainability Climate science International development Environmental policy Biology Natural resource management Communications or marketing (for outreach roles) Business or finance (for sustainability investment roles) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Skills and Competencies Applicants are expected to demonstrate: Strong interest in environmental conservation Passion for sustainability and climate action Research and analytical skills Teamwork and collaboration abilities Communication skills Organizational and time management skills Graduate-level internships may require strong research skills and analytical capabilities. Internship Structure The WWF BRIDGE Internship lasts 10 weeks during the summer, typically running from June to August. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Work Format Internships may be offered in different formats: Virtual internships Hybrid internships On-site placements This flexible structure allows students from different regions to participate. Workload Approximately 35 hours per week Full-time summer commitment Interns collaborate with WWF teams on real-world projects that contribute directly to the organization s mission. Professional Development Opportunities One of the most valuable aspects of the BRIDGE Internship is the extensive professional development offered to participants. These opportunities include: Leadership training Career mentoring Resume building workshops Interview preparation sessions Networking sessions with WWF leadership Professional certification programs ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Participants also gain access to the Cornell University eCornell training program, which offers online professional education courses. These development activities help interns build a strong foundation for careers in conservation, sustainability, and environmental policy. Program Impact Since its launch, the BRIDGE Internship has become one of the most impactful programs for early-career conservationists. Key achievements include: Thousands of applicants from around the world Over 180 alumni working in conservation-related careers Many interns later returning to work at WWF or other environmental organizations. The program plays a critical role in helping students from diverse backgrounds enter the conservation sector. Who Can Apply The WWF BRIDGE Internship is designed primarily for students and early-career professionals who are interested in environmental conservation and sustainability. Eligible Applicants The program accepts applicants who are currently enrolled in: Associate degree programs Bachelor s degree programs Master s degree programs Doctoral programs (with limited coursework completed) Applicants must be active students at the time of application. Doctoral Student Requirement Doctoral students must not have completed more than two years of coursework to qualify for the internship. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Application Requirements Applicants must prepare the following documents: Required Documents Resume or Curriculum Vitae (CV) Cover Letter explaining interest in the internship Letter of recommendation (requested for final candidates) The cover letter should clearly demonstrate the applicant s passion for conservation, sustainability, and environmental impact. How to Apply To apply for the WWF BRIDGE Internship: Visit the official WWF careers website. Search for available BRIDGE internship positions. Select the internship that matches your skills and interests. Submit your application with the required documents. Available positions are usually posted on the WWF careers page starting in late March each year. Apply here Application Deadline Applications typically open in late March , with many listings closing around May 2026 depending on the specific internship role. Why You Should Apply The WWF BRIDGE Internship is one of the most valuable opportunities for students who want to build a career in environmental sustainability. Key reasons to apply include: Paid internship with competitive hourly wages Hands-on conservation experience Networking with global environmental leaders Professional training and career development Exposure to real-world environmental challenges Opportunity to work with one of the most respected conservation organizations in the world For students passionate about protecting biodiversity, fighting climate change, and shaping sustainable policies, the BRIDGE Internship provides a powerful entry point into the environmental sector. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(139); }); For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post WWF BRIDGE Paid Summer Internship 2026: Fully Paid Global Internship Opportunity for Students Interested in Conservation Careers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
